It has a heavy duty zipper that is backed by a flap of fabric so you don't catch any chest hair on the way up and down. After paddling 4 hours this afternoon the zipper stayed in place without working its way down. The arm holes seem a little small but that adds to the effectiveness of the insulation qualities. I could feel the fabric at the arm holes while paddling but did not get any chafing. Next time out I will try wearing a lightweight wicking shirt under the vest just to see if it feels any different. This item is priced pretty closely with other items of its type, although some I have tried did not have the protective flap of fabric backing the zipper. If this thing falls apart on me I will try to edit this review but for now I am very pleased with this purchase.
We may have gotten a bad batch or something, but it really didn't work.
Honestly can't beat these for what they cost. Paired instantly with my phone, sound is surprisingly clear, and the ear hooks stay put during workouts. Great budget pick.
Bought this for a weekend project and ended up using it constantly. Solid build, works perfectly right out of the box. Totally worth it.
This had a lot of good tips and basic information on how to survive, was a good little book that I enjoyed very much

I've had this paperback for about two years now and it's held up surprisingly well through multiple moves and lending it to friends. The spine hasn't cracked badly and pages are still intact. The story itself is twisted and clever, not my absolute favorite thriller ever but definitely memorable. Took me a bit to get into but the second half redeemed it. Solid read that survives heavy use.
Worth every penny, works better than expected.
I've reread this trilogy three times now and the paperback is still holding up great despite being tossed in my bag constantly. Goleman's worldbuilding hooked me instantly.
This a well constructed sandal except for the arch. But overall it is a durable and fashionable sandal.

Some may enjoy this overpriced series. I did not. These books are badly in need of an editor and a more interesting main character. Lexi Graves is shallow, often silly, and altogether a bit of a bore. Lexi also has a really hard time figuring out who the villain is until she has missed many of the clues, messed up the investigation, and gotten herself into a desperate situation that she then has to be rescued from. Although the secondary characters are well-drawn, and the plots are interesting enough, the series as a whole does not justify the price. I paid for the set, based on the good reviews, but I was having a difficult time reading past all the grammar mistakes and language oddities until I hit on the happy solution of bookmarking all the errors as if I were grading a paper. By the end of the third book I had more than 100 bookmarks.

I've been reading this series since the nineties and have always loved how Peter and Rina's personal story weaves through the cases. This latest installment is a decent read but didn't blow me away. The mystery itself is fine, there's enough going on to keep you turning pages, but the pacing drags in the middle and I figured out the twist well before the reveal. What's missing is the deeper character development that made the earlier books so special. Rina feels like an afterthought here, and even Peter seems to be going through the motions. It's not bad by any means, just average. Longtime fans will probably enjoy catching up with the characters, but this won't be the one that hooks new readers.

When I bought this it was under fifteen dollars, which is very reasonable for any sort of timer light switch product. I installed it in my bathroom to control the exhaust fan and it works perfectly. You need to know what you're doing in the junction box because all 4 wires need to be hooked up correctly (and there -must- be a white wire for power,) but it was not difficult and it looks nice along side the other switches.
Worked fine for about two months, then the grinding mechanism cracked and stopped working entirely. Cheap plastic inside. Don't waste your money.

Got these for my Fluval and the sponge material is seriously dense compared to the stock ones. Water runs crystal clear now and my filter is way quieter too. The carbon packets are a nice bonus for when I need to swap them out. If you've got a turtle tank or a heavy-bioload aquarium, don't hesitate. Way cheaper than buying OEM and the quality is just as good if not better.
Love all three titles in this series. If you are already a fan of Roberts, it won't disappoint. If you aren't a fan you will be. Narrator does a nice job.

I've reread this entire Mackenzie series at least three times now and my Kindle copy is still going strong. The bookmarking holds up perfectly even with constant highlighting and note-taking. These characters never get old. Absolutely worth keeping in your permanent library.

Couldn't put this down! The character development throughout the series is incredible. Shan really knows how to pull you in and keep you invested. That ending was everything I needed. Worth every penny.
For the price, this little rasp is unbeatable. Tears through wood quickly and the handle is comfortable. Replacement blades are cheap too. Totally worth it.
My son is thomas fan number one and this track is very happy spends hours and hours playing very shock resistant material is recommended easy to assemble very happy with what you purchased thanks

Bought this thinking it might offer some interesting behind-the-scenes takes on his interviews. Nope. It's basically just O'Reilly patting himself on the back for being "tough" while lobging softballs at people he agrees with and talking over everyone else. The whole "no-spin" claim is almost funny given how much spin is packed into every chapter. I made it about halfway through before I just couldn't justify spending any more time on it. The writing itself is pretty bland too, reads like a rushed transcript dump more than an actual book. Honestly expected at least some decent anecdotes from someone who's talked to that many public figures, but there's nothing here you haven't already heard on his show. Returned it the next day. Don't waste your money.
scratchy sound on both movies. tape hiss and pops on both. good old movies. picture quality is good on both movies

Arrived two days earlier than promised and the packaging was seriously impressive. Double-boxed with dense foam molding around every component, so nothing was rattling around or scratched up. Pulled it out and the first thing I noticed was how substantial everything feels. The main body has real heft to it and the clamping mechanism operates smoothly with zero play. Hardware was individually bagged and labeled, which saved me a ton of frustration during setup. Bolted it to my bench grinder in about twenty minutes and the adjustments are rock solid. Honestly, for what this costs compared to the big name alternatives, the build quality is outstanding. Already sharpened three chisels and a plane blade this afternoon. Very impressed.

Been using these on our basement and garage doors for about eight months now, and they've held up way better than I expected. My three-year-old yanks on them constantly and there's no cracking or loosening so far. The plastic is thick and doesn't flex much, which is honestly what I was worried about when I first opened the package. Installation took maybe two minutes per knob. Only reason I'm not giving five stars is that they're a bit awkward for adults to grip too, you really have to squeeze in a specific spot, and guests always struggle with it. But that's kind of the point, right? Overall, solid build quality and they do exactly what they're supposed to. Would buy again without hesitation.
Santa brought this gift and my daughter refuses to put her down the hair is so soft. This baby doll really is just the prettiest little thing

Got this a few weeks back and have already put it through heavy use. Build quality feels way better than I expected for the cost, no rattles or loose parts straight out of the box. It handles regular wear and tear without a scratch so far. Shipping was quick too. Honestly can't complain about anything at this point. Great value and does exactly what it promises.

Bought this months ago and have reread it three times already on my Kindle. Holds up brilliantly even with constant page-turning and highlighting. The plot twists still get me every single time. Solid writing that doesn't fade after multiple reads. Definitely worth keeping in your permanent library.

Picked this up on sale and honestly glad I didn't pay full price. The production values are undeniably gorgeous - every frame looks like a painting and the period detail is impeccable. Brody carries the whole thing on his shoulders and delivers a quietly powerful lead performance. Problem is the pacing meanders badly in the second act and Polanski seems more interested in atmosphere than actually moving the story forward. Several scenes drag on way longer than they need to and by the hour-forty mark I was checking my watch. For fifteen bucks it's a decent addition to the collection if you enjoy slow-burn period pieces, but at the regular thirty-dollar retail price I'd say rent it first. Beautiful but overlong and self-indulgent.

Grabbed this on a whim and honestly can't believe I waited so long. My charging cables used to slide right off the edge of my desk every single night, which drove me nuts. Now they stay exactly where I need them. The build feels solid and it doesn't budge when you plug in. Small enough to tuck anywhere without looking cluttered. For the price, this is one of those purchases that just quietly makes daily life a little less annoying.

I owned this movie as a child (purchased at Musicland in the basement of Westroads Mall Omaha, NE) and was excited to see it again but I must admit that I enjoyed the Japanese version better. Despite the fact that the main character was dubbed by an Asian Actor (Keye Luke who portrayed Master Po on Kung Fu and was also the voice of the main characterin RODAN) it just seems to flow better and make more sense. I also appreciate the original soundtrack for the film as it seems to fit the mood or tone of the film. With special faetures and two different versions of the film I would say this is a bargain at twice the price. So do yourself a favor and pick it up before it goes out of print.
Some how I changed the filter with this but indication for carbon on machine never go away

Ordered these for my brother's birthday and they arrived two days early in a nice box with no damage. Pulled them out and was immediately impressed by the color and feel. He tried them on right away and they fit perfectly.
Couldn't put this down, finished it in two nights. Perfect for anyone who loves twisty thrillers with unreliable narrators. My book club is reading it next.

I've had this on my Kindle for months now and keep coming back to reread my favorite chapters. The story holds up beautifully even after multiple reads, which says a lot. Heath and Allie's journey hits different each time. Still obsessed after all this time. Definitely worth the purchase.

I purchased this at Bed Bath and Beyond 15 months ago. It squeegees the glass very well, and the suction cup fixture stays up. (It detached just once, in the seasonal change from humid to dry weather.) No rust, and the rubber blade has stayed flexible. The folding design keeps the squeegee off to the side, out of sight. Yet, I'm unhappy: the folding pivot point has loosened over these 15 months. If it was just a bit "wiggly," that might be okay. However, the pivot and its fold-lock mechanism are obviously parting ways. The squeegee is no longer effective because the blade cannot be held perpendicular at the end of the swiveling handle. My experience with this squeegee is similar to other "simplehuman" products I've used in the past. They look well-designed, with an aesthetic similar to Oxo and other neo-design products. But, their engineering has invariably incorporated flaws that eventually destroy the product's usefulness. I'll be replacing this squeegee with one from another manufacturer, and not purchasing from this line again.

Very nice micro case but could use some improvements, primarily on the hard drive cage. Overall this case is easy to work with as most parts unscrew and can be removed such as the top and the motherboard tray. Wiring can be tricky so a modular power supply and shorter SATA cables can pay off here. Lots of room for your CPU heatsink of choice and a large fan blowing from the front that cools well even at very low RPM's. The two main complaints I have for this case are the orientation of the hard drive cage which should be rotated 90 and the lack of sound deadening materials in the side panels. Otherwise this is a great case that is very compact for what it holds and rewards you for your efforts.

first, let's talk about shipping is amazing fast! I order it a day before Thanksgiving and it arrived today noon, a day after Thanksgiving. so the shipping rate is amazing. it wasn't thanksgiving it probably arrived yesterday. ( it may take longer in different places, I'm from NYC ) second, this battery grip is made by Canon, so it feels and matches the body identical. if you are wondering how is feels about the quality, then you should go ahead and grab the grip on your DSLR. the feeling that you get from it is the feeling that you expect from the battery grip. (mine is Canon 50D) * This product comes with a 6 AA battery tray so no need to spend your money to get it. the battery grip is made in Japan. Installation is easy, takes less then a minute. plus it has an instruction in it. after installed it my 50D looks a lot more professional but camera with the grip plus a Canon flash, it feels a lot heavier. but i can deal with it, and it is not that a big deal. over all good product and i would recommend it

The locking mechanism on this thing is no joke. My labrador used to raid the trash every single day, but after three weeks he still hasn't cracked it. Love that you can flip the lock off easily when you're actually throwing stuff away. Wish it held a little more but the raccoons in my house don't stand a chance.

Bought this for my kids and they have fun on it, but I'm pretty disappointed with the quality. After about eight months the stitching on the mat started unraveling and the foam covering is splitting apart. For the price I really expected something sturdier. It's not terrible while it lasts, but spending this much on a trampoline that can't survive regular use beyond a single season is frustrating. Honestly wish I had gone with a different brand.
Paid $4.99 for this and honestly it reads like a rough first draft. The plot jumps around constantly and none of the characters feel real. Not worth even a dollar.

Not a bad little device. I have owned this MP3 player for several days now and it is everything I expected it to be. Loading music is astonishingly easy. the 15 GB of storage will take me quite a while to fill up. There is an SD card slot that I don't expect that I will ever have reason to use. I did not test the device with an SD card but I shook it down in every other way. I've dropped it a few times (not intentionally) and it survived intact. The audio quality is fine and the radio adequate. I mostly listen to my local college station which is within ten miles of my home, it plays that very well. The scroll through menu is logical and easy to read. My only negatives were that it is virtually impossible to read in bright sunlight and the menu when going from MP3 player to other features is not as straightforward as I would like. Also MP3 player is the default mode. If you turn it off set to the radio, when you turn it on again it will be set in MP3 mode. That is not really a bug, but it is a bit of an annoyance. All around it is well worth four stars.

Package arrive within 3 days from East coast and I am on West coast. Box contained a plastic bag labeled as 12 ea 2" 13 Ball Nylon rollers (they are actually measured as 1- 3/4" for a 2" track). The bag had been slit open as if someone wanted to check out the product, but all 12 rollers were the same. The nylon rollers are sealed on both sides so it is almost impossible to see how many ball bearings there actually are. Product has been installed and are quiet as the door opens and closes.

Got this for my daughter's first birthday and she's obsessed. The different textures on each page keep her entertained way longer than regular board books do. She especially goes crazy for the crinkly parts and anything she can grab and pull on. My favorite feature is that most of the pieces are tethered so I'm not constantly fishing things out from under the couch. The cow on the cover comes off with velcro though, and that one's already gone missing a few times. Construction is holding up decently after about two months of daily use. One stitching spot is starting to fray a little but nothing major. Overall a solid busy book for the car seat or diaper bag. Would definitely recommend for the 6-18 month crowd.
For the price you can't beat these. My daughter makes bracelets constantly and we still have plenty left. Colors are vibrant and they don't snap easily.

I ordered 2 of these for my kids. The bands are challenging to stretch over the mattress far enough so that when you pull the opposite side to do that band the other end pulls up from the opposite side- not all the way off, but it still is a back and forth. Today after 2 months of use one of the elastic corner bands came apart from the mattress pad. It already broke and I've only taken it off the bed 3 times. A total waste of money. Get the kind that is like a sheet and goes all the way down. I was hoping I could return these and get my money back but my return window closed 2 weeks ago. One of them has never even been out of the package.
Burns the hell out of my hands. Doesn't work at high temps
Started strong but got way too preachy and self-indulgent by the end.

The elastic waistband is surprisingly stretchy and holds up well after multiple washes. Fabric feels a bit thin but breathes nicely in warm weather. Sizing definitely runs a size small, so order up. Prints are cute enough for the price.
Reads like a cautionary tale that hits close to home. The fictional approach works well even if I wanted more historical depth. Finished it in two sittings and kept thinking about it afterward.
My Yorkies love it! And, I love it because it is so light! No weight to it at all. My 'girls' needed that extra step to get up on my pillow top bed.

Been buying these freeze-dried blocks for about six months now and they hold up way better than other brands I've tried. Even with daily scooping and my two heavy cats trampling all over them, they barely break down into dust like the cheaper stuff does. I store the bag in a cabinet and the blocks stay solid and fresh, never going stale or absorbing weird smells from the pantry. My orange tabby is incredibly picky and he actually gets excited at mealtime now, which says a lot. Rehydrates fast with warm water and doesn't turn to mush even if I leave it out a bit. The smell is actually pleasant compared to pate cans. Worth every penny.
I do not Pendulum Summon, so sue me. But as a disruptive card and a Level 7 I can bring out whenever, why not?

Meena Harper is a typical woman living in NYC - she picks the wrong men, says the wrong things (aka predicts people's deaths), and attracts vampires. Okay, so maybe she isn't a typical NYC woman, but what she is is a fun character. In this sequel to Insatiable, readers are welcomed back into her life six months after the first novel ends. As the novel opens, readers see Meena as a serious agent for the Palatine. Her brother Jon is still trying to get a job with the Palatine. Alaric still wants Meena. Lucien still wants to make Meena his bride. And that's just the start of it. There is a rising vampire army hunting Meena; there is the Palatine hunting Lucien; and there is something "off" about the new members of the Palatine. With a fast-paced plot and quite a few twists and turns throughout, Cabot has written an intriguing sequel. If you enjoyed Insatiable, you will not be disappointed with Overbite. On a final note, I just want to say how much I really enjoy Cabot's taking on vampirism. She is clever, funny, and satirical, making these some of my favorite Cabot novels. This is the reason I gave it 5 stars instead of 4.

This is a perfect example of CGI taking over. It's obvious someone drew up a storyboard with a dozen action scenes and another dozen tense lines. There may have been vague intentions to add in credible plot details and character motivations before the actors arrived. Time apparently ran out. This Lex Luthor is the weakest villain of all time...just incredibly bland and flat. The actors are given so little to work with that scenes must be propelled with clenched teeth, furrowed brows, and pained brooding. None of them seem like they could have possibly had a minute of fun in their whole lives. The color palette is dreary and limited. The musical cues are mind numbingly predictable. The camera angles are never inventive. Except for a couple of new visuals, it's so much of the same old same old. That's unforgiveable considering how many actors have now played Bruce Wayne and Clark Kent. If either of them show up in some other form or fashion I would beg Hollywood to stop any and all references to their past. It's becoming a xerox of a xerox.

I picked this up because everyone said it would make me cry, and I love dog stories. The concept of seeing a family's whole journey through a dog's eyes is genuinely clever, and Enzo's voice works well in the first few chapters. But about halfway through, the racing metaphors got exhausting. Every single life event gets compared to driving in the rain, and it stops being profound after the tenth time. The custody battle subplot felt overly dramatic and manipulative, like the author was just trying to force an emotional reaction. I didn't hate it, but I expected way more based on the hype. The ending was sweet enough, but getting there was a chore. Just okay for me.

If you think small town living is safer than big cities think again. THis is the story of two innocent girls who went to the grocery store and never returned home. The were picked up by two local teenagers raped and then thrown over a 150 foot bridge. The story pulls you in never lets go. If this tale wasn't documented you would think it was written by Clive Barker. five stars for one the best true crime stories ever published. Ask Ann Rule she will tell you. Why are still reading this review? Buy the book!!
The night vision on this thing is surprisingly clear. Caught everything on camera without any blurry footage. Super happy with it.
Very pleased with the quality of this cute little train set, the detail and colors makes it a very cute toy train.
Arrived two days early, nicely boxed with no dents. Unpacked it and immediately hung it up. Super cute and sturdy for my clothespins!

This is a very good LED lamp for the price. Most people will find the light a bit harsh and the range of color temperatures a bit arbitrary. People who like to fiddle with stuff will enjoy this lamp. Let me list the pros and cons. Pros: The lamp is lightweight and seems pretty sturdy and stable on the base. The light is very steady and there is no flicker. The controls are easy to learn and access. The finish is nice. The lamp head is long enough for most desktop applications. The lamp head can be pivoted and tilted to a wide range of angles. The head can be pointed backward to bounce the light off the wall for an indirect lighting effect. Cons: The lamp head can only be rotated 45 degrees left and right on the base. The two middle color temperatures are quite similar and the whitest temperature is very harsh and almost bluish. The lamp uses a power block instead of a direct plug in. The styling of the lamp probably won't fit with most desk styles. It is sort of nouveu but also sort of sharp angled. I will be using the lamp for some photographic work and it is very good for setting white balance and exposure for close ups. My camera can really tell the difference in the colors, and the range of dimming is very wide. I am not sure whether the lamp has any eye protecting value as stated, or not. But at least you never have to change a light bulb! Good value for money and a recommended product.

I use lens hoods to improve contrast, as well as to provide a bit of physical protection for the lens, since I don't believe in adding a "protective" filter to the front of a lens, except where there is a chemical or severe debris risk. That said, this one is pretty small, so not much protection physically, but if it helps increase contrast, then it will be worth the investment.

I used this in marking a field and soil sampling. Did awesome. It's not sub-meter but seems to have accuracy of 6-8 feet. Magnet is strong, worked flawlessly with a dell venue 8 pro. Cable is long enough, I know that can be problematic. Acquired Sat signal and held it on a cloudy day. Very few skips or signal loss. I could drive as fast as I wanted without loss of signal.

that such a simple little thing would help me so much!? This has a very nice weight to it, so it stays in place. It's not HEAVY, but it's enough to keep it where you put it. I had two cords on my nightstand that i had to wedge between the clock and the lamp, or they would fall behind the nightstand. This solved the problem nicely. I love Quirky items. Browse their stuff.

The one's we received had the green light button on the right not the left as pictured. Which turns out is exactly what we wanted. So the picture seems to be mirrored left to right. Works great though. If you plan on using several you might want to make a chart of how you set the four dip switches. When I was testing my handy work I switched the light on and off several times than sat down to rest. The light then began flashing on and off on its own. I was confused until my son came upstairs and told me his light was turning on and off. Only then I realized I set both to the same code accidentally so it was turning on and off both lights.
This is my second Eureka Enviro steamer. My first one died (it was a used one). It works really well, and I am happy with it.
Honestly didn't expect much for the price but this thing blows me away. Way better than similar figures I've paid double for. Solid buy.

I really love this series and all the books by this author. The rhyming is great and the illustrations are adorable! Kids will love these books and I hope there will be many more. I can't wait to read them to my nieces and nephews. Well done.
What a neat thing! I can fit several bills in it and it looks and feels like a real bolt. I'm a retired mechanic and thought there's no way this could ever fool me.
Love this series was one of the best I have read. The author has out done her self with details n keeping u interested in the book n series.
I liked better the other ones. All Access and Broken Sound were far better! This one has a lot of loose ends and it's not really as good as the others.

I have had Drinkwells for years my cats and dog love them but after many years the cat one stopped.Thought I would try something different for a change. - it has no fountain like the drinkwell.Maybe my fault for not realizing that. Water comes out over the dome. They and i miss the fountain effect.It is very noisy if you let water get low which is very easy with 4 cats. Their is no resovior option so if you have a few animals it does not last It has a tiny hole to fill the water. My cats do not seem to be thrilled with it and go to the Bigdog Drinkwell now. I think if you are used to the drinkwell you may not like this. I will be buying them a drinkwell again. Yes it is a bit easier to clean and I like how it collects any debris but... Drinkwell it isn't!
Swapped out all my old kitchen hardware for these and what a difference. Great weight, beautiful finish, and they installed in minutes. Totally transformed my cabinets.

Bought a four-pack of these last year and honestly forgot about them until Hurricane Beryl knocked out our power for a solid week. These little things are absolute lifesavers. The feature that really sold me is the magnetic back, I stuck one right on the fridge door and it lit up my entire kitchen. No more holding a flashlight in my mouth while trying to make a sandwich. The beam throw is honestly surprising for something this compact. I used one every single night for seven days straight on the same set of batteries and it never dimmed. They feel cheap in the hand but who cares when they perform like this. Just ordered another pack for my parents' house and the garage. For the price you really can't go wrong.

Got this for my daughter's birthday since she loves anything rodeo-related. The pieces feel sturdy enough and the artwork is decent, but honestly we've only played it twice and she's already lost interest. The gameplay gets repetitive fast, basically just go around the board, buy event spaces, collect money. Nothing really exciting or interactive happens. My kids kept asking when something fun was going to happen and I didn't have an answer. For the price I expected more variety or some twist that makes it stand out. We've got other themed monopoly-style games that get pulled out regularly, but this one is already sitting on the shelf gathering dust. It's not terrible, just kind of flat and forgettable. Wouldn't really recommend unless you find it on clearance.

2 stars for the fact that it REALLY is a MAGIC LIFT as advertised. Being returned because of how it fits however. I routinely wear Lilyette Minimizers and Champion Sports bras, so I am used to different ways of supporting the girls. With this bra, fitted by their instructions, the cup absolutely LIFTED, but everything spilled out the sides, leaving the areas of the top of the cup empty/large. No way to explain it, but it also did not separate well, so center of bra did not lay against chest. Clothing made me look high and pointy. I have none of these issues with the other bras, so this just isn't a fit for me -- 36D, mid 50's.

I honestly didn't expect much from this cash register given how many noisy toys we already have, but it's been a pleasant surprise. The number recognition feature is actually helping my four year old practice counting, which is more than I can say for most of the gadgets cluttering our playroom. The scanner beeps realistically and the coin slot works smoothly without jamming like cheaper alternatives we've tried. My son loves playing store and handing me receipts from the little drawer. The volume has two settings which is a lifesaver because the songs get repetitive fast. Only minor complaint is the included play food feels flimsy, but the register itself has survived being dropped on tile twice already. For the price point, definitely exceeded what I was anticipating.

Picked this up on sale and honestly it's worth every penny. Charges my batteries while streaming Spotify from my phone all day on the job. Speakers get plenty loud over the saws and the AUX port has held up fine after six months of daily use.

I hadn't seen this movie in years and then I found it on the Instant Video list and had to watch it again. It's a really fun, sweet story and a nice change from the mostly trashy films/tv shows that are out now. I'm used to seeing Timothy Dalton do more serious story lines but he proves he can do comedy very well.
Love these Jockey socks! The red seam on the toes of the black socks & blue seam on the blue pairs eases my washing & my husband's dressing routine in the morning.
Twelve self-contained stories of mysteries solved, some criminal. Entertaining, but the need to wrap up the story in one chapter prevents character development and more interesting twists and turns.

I remember the 12 inch Indiana Jones doll back in 1981 that was out for about a week, it seemed anyway. It was imposable to find then and I have been waiting for it for years! (Because I wouldn't pay the ridiculous price for it as time went by). Now with the new movie coming out, thank GOD, here come the Indy figures and dolls! (As nerdy as that sounds). I chose this one over the talking Indy simply because it looks more like Indiana than the other does. It isn't the old 81 doll I remember as a kid but it'll do, for now. The detail is nice as are the accessories. Unfortunately it has "whip cracking action" which means if you move a lever on his back his arm moves the whip up and down. Does anyone at Hasbro have the slightest clue how useless this feature is? It's a minor nuisance and overall it's worth owning though other companies are now making much better detailed versions of Indiana Jones in 12" form.
Perfect gift for my sister who loves casual everyday jewelry.
Well written! I enjoyed it very much and will read more by I H Laking. Intriguing new steampunk world and characters.

Good Quality bag, should hold up well. Like the looks of it. Tapered front fits under seat well. Strap on rear for tail light is nice. I don't care for some bags out there that have all kinds of outside straps, bottle holders, pockets, etc. This one is more streamlined with your stuff stored inside the bag, not held somehow on the outside. And the inside is roomy! Held back on a 5 star rating for several reasons. One, it's a little pricey. Forty bucks is a lot for a bag to store stuff on the back of your bike. And secondly the design of how the top opens up could be better. The top is held closed by two zippers down each side, plus a velcro closure across the front. The velcro flap is located under your seat, where it is hard to get to. And it's strong velcro, so you really need to get a firm grip on it. And then the zippers are held together with a strap, the idea being that you can pull on the strap and open both zippers at the same time. In theory this sounds good but reaching the strap, which is also under the seat, is not the easiest thing to do either. I ended up cutting off the strap holding the 2 zippers together. This helps quite a bit. Now I can quickly open the bag just on one side without having to undo the velcro. And you can pretty much grab what you want with just one zipper open. IF the zippers opened from the rear, and the velcro closure was across the rear, then it would be an ideal bag and maybe I wouldn't feel like forty bucks was too much for it.
For the price, these are unbeatable. Fits right over my prescription frames with zero hassle.
It was good while it lasted. Perfect fit, worked in our Nikon charger. But after a year and a half it would not take a charge and we had to replace it.
Got this for my husband's birthday and he's watched it three times already. The case is pretty flimsy but the discs have held up great after months of use. No scratches at all.

Bought this for my son's birthday and honestly wish I'd returned it immediately. The whole thing is a letdown from start to finish. Assembly took way longer than expected and the instructions were basically useless. Once we finally got it together, the battery barely holds a charge, maybe 20 minutes of driving before it dies, and then you're stuck waiting hours for it to recharge. My boy is average height and still struggles to reach the foot pedal comfortably, so he ends up just sitting there frustrated. Steering is clunky and stiff, and it gets stuck on even slightly uneven grass. He's asked to ride it maybe twice since his birthday and now it's just taking up garage space. Save your cash and look at something else.

Bought these for a rainy kayak trip and stayed completely dry the whole time. The material is surprisingly lightweight but totally waterproof. Wore them over shorts and they didn't stick or get uncomfortable. I'd highly recommend these to anyone who fishes, hikes, or works outdoors in wet weather. Worth every penny for sure.
Sturdy, holds all my Camis and makes them easy to access.

Bought these last summer for our backyard patio and really wanted to love them. Out of the box, all four lit up fine and looked great for the first month. Then the problems started. One by one they started flickering and dying, even after full days of charging in direct sunlight. By month three, only one was still working. The metal poles also rusted at the connection points where they screw together, which is pretty disappointing for something designed to stay outdoors. The plastic tops cracked after a few weeks of sun exposure too. These just aren't built to handle actual weather. I've had similar solar torches from other brands that lasted years, but these barely survived a single season. Don't waste your money.

I Finally received my buff and I really like the design and color. I will say that once I let the seller know my Buff had never arrived, he was very apologetic and immediately sent out another one which arrived in just a couple of days, just in time for some really hot weather! One thing I do need to mention about wearing a Buff, I found if I wash and condition my hair with products that make my hair very soft and smooth, the buff won't stay on for very long, especially if I am working outside It slides off and I keep having to readjust it. i I am just using a "plain" shampoo and minimal conditioner on the ends that seems to solve the problem., What i love is the buff idoes a great job keeping hair out of the way with out making me sweat more! Worth it If you like to work outside.
Bought this as a backup for my XC tripod and honestly the quality is just as good as the original. At this price, it's a no-brainer. Fits perfectly and works like a charm.

Arrived a day earlier than expected and the box was in perfect condition, no dents or crushed corners which is always a good sign. Everything was neatly packed inside with the dock snug in a fitted foam cutout. First impressions are solid, it has a nice weight to it and doesn't feel cheap. The aluminum finish looks sleek on my desk and the ports have a satisfying click when you plug into them. Hooked it up to my setup right away and my laptop recognized two monitors immediately with zero fuss. Knocking off one star because the included power adapter is surprisingly bulky and takes up too much room on my power strip. Otherwise, really happy with the purchase so far and it's been running flawlessly for the past week.

After about three months of light reading, the spine on my copy is already splitting and several pages are coming loose. The glue just doesn't hold up. For a paperback at this price point, I expected better construction. The story itself is fine if you want something undemanding, but honestly I'm frustrated that mine is falling apart before I even finished it. Wouldn't repurchase based on how cheaply made this feels.
This is on our "Top 25" movie list. Superbly acted, written, edited, filmed, etc. Sound is terrific too. That's meaningful because of the... well, you just have to see it for yourself. Twice.

It does what it's supposed to do, reads my drives and transfers data without issues. I've used it with a couple of 3.5" HDDs and a 2.5" SSD, and the read speeds over USB 3.0 are solid. My main gripe is the build quality. The plastic feels thin and the drive slot has some wiggle room that makes me nervous every time I insert or pull out a drive. The power button is also oddly placed on the back, which is annoying if you keep it on your desk and want to power it down without reaching around. For the price, it's an acceptable tool for occasional use, but I wouldn't trust it for heavy daily swapping. It gets the job done, just don't expect premium construction.
I bought this and additional Legos for a Christmas gifts

I expected more tension given the premise, but everything resolved a bit too neatly. Emma barely struggled and the historical setting felt glossed over. Still a decent quick read for fans of the genre.

The company that supplied this "used" MBPr was extremely helpful. It wasn't really a used machine as far as I can tell. This came in the original box (more on that later) and shrunk wrapped. When I placed the order, it wasn't supposed to arrive for 4-5 days. I contacted the seller and asked if they could please ship it quicker. I also mentioned I would pay whatever the additional charge would be. They replied quickly, had it delivered in two days (NY to NJ) and there was no additional charge. I was thrilled. That is until I saw the laptop box. It was in bad shape. Not horrible, but one of the corners (and under the corner) was severely damaged. Crinkled and partially crushed. Enough so that it caused some concern about the laptops possible condition when I opened it. Turns out the laptop was in flawless condition, so no biggie. The box is still useable (although damaged) for eventual resale. I'm assuming the box was damaged BEFORE they sent it since the shipping box that contained the laptop and it's box was in great condition. Hmmm.... Anyway, it was listed as used, but there wasn't a scratch, ding or dent of any kind. Bottom line, I got a great machine with no damage for a great price with excellent customer service. No complaints. Just wish the laptop box wasn't damaged. That's the only reason I gave them 4 stars instead of 5. Highly recommend!! Great experience overall.

I purchased this game for my 5 year old. Just like the original Operation game, it's fun and surprising to play. This Spongebob version plays just like the original. I really does help a child with his or her hand to eye coordination. A plus for younger kids learning to write, it allows good motor skill and muscle memory for the fingers and assist in the development of holding a pencil to write. We play this game as a family and have fun taking turns and razzing each other after setting the buzzer off.

Both my kittens go absolutely nuts batting this thing across the kitchen floor. They've scratched and chewed on it for three straight weeks now and the seams are still totally intact, which honestly surprised me. Wish it had a little more weight to it, but it holds up great.

This one is a 4 star read also! This one picks up right where the other left off. This is another short read but you won't be able to put it down. Of course you get more insight on Nick and Becca. And she's still a firecracker and I'm loving it! I wish the 3rd was already out so I can see how this series goes, can't wait for it! And the ending to this one OMG, what is gonna happen. I really wish these books were longer cause you really get caught up in the storyline of it. Would definitely recommend this series!
Excellent jacket shirt a bit larger than I expected though.

Snagged this during a lightning deal and honestly couldn't be happier. The rose gold tone is subtle and classy, not gaudy like some pieces I've seen. Feels way more expensive than what I paid. Wore it every day since it arrived and still looks brand new. Definitely worth every penny.
Bought this for my daughter two Decembers ago and it's still holding up great despite being read constantly every holiday season. Pages haven't torn, binding is solid. Wonderful nostalgic gift.

Ordered this for a nostalgia binge and it arrived two days earlier than expected. The case was in perfect shape with no cracks, which is honestly rare for DVD sets shipped in bubble mailers. All three discs were secure in their slots and played without any skipping. Picture quality looks solid for a show from the early 70s and the menus load fast. My kids had never seen it and were hooked within the first episode. The Hawaii trip episodes alone make this season worth picking up. Really happy with this purchase overall and planning to grab season five next.

Picked this up for my kid's birthday and honestly expected better for what they're charging. The build quality feels flimsy and I can already tell it won't survive more than a few months of regular play. The plastic is that thin hollow type that cracks if you look at it wrong. My son was excited for about ten minutes before one of the attachments stopped clicking into place properly. For close to thirty bucks I'd expect something way sturdier than this. You're basically paying for the brand name and the flashy packaging. I've seen similar toys at the dollar store with comparable quality. Wouldn't repurchase and honestly wish I'd spent the money on something else entirely.
Thank you. I received the book I ordered in a timely manner, and in the condition described.
Absolutely love this thing. Dinner practically cooks itself and the lightweight design makes cleanup a breeze.
Great product! Put into my first build. I'm pleased with the results.

Annamae runs away on her wedding day and ends up barging in on an undercover cop who is in hiding, finding her Granny in an old folks home and learning more about her Mom, Step dad and 'out of the picture before she was born' dad. Oh did I forget to mention her family's life is documented on TV every day? interesting and fun read.

Worked fine for the first four months or so. Suction was decent, nothing mind-blowing but okay for quick crumbs on the counter. Then right around the five month mark the motor just started whining and dying mid-use. Now it won't even turn on at all. I take good care of my stuff too, never dropped it, always kept it on the charger properly. For what these cost there's no excuse for it quitting this fast. My old Black and Decker dustbuster lasted nearly eight years before the battery finally gave out. This thing didn't even make it half a year of light weekend use. Wife is furious I spent this much on it. Going back to a basic brand and saving myself 200 bucks next time. Complete waste of money.
Still hilarious after reading it three times. The humor holds up perfectly. Can't wait for the sequel to drop!
A little tricky to fill with air without letting a bunch out while trying to get the plug in. But fun! Also, I don't know why it came out of box with a sticky/greasy residue all over it.

Bought these for my 10-month-old thinking they'd be bigger based on the photos. Each one fits entirely in my palm. That said, they're surprisingly loud for the size and my son goes crazy shaking them around. The seam where the two halves meet feels a little rough on one of them but nothing sharp. For the price I can't complain too much. Definitely better than the plastic maracas we had before because these actually make a decent sound.

Perfect for my wife who struggles with heavy drills. The 8V motor handles basic household tasks without wearing her out. Great gift for anyone needing lightweight tools for small jobs around the house.
works great & the charge kept up all day long

Finally after years of fighting algae we have a clear pond! It took a couple weeks but it has been perfect since then; we couldn't be happier. We have tried so many different things to clear up our pond and if I had known how well this worked we would have bought it years ago.
Decent way to kill an afternoon. The ensemble has solid chemistry and a few scenes genuinely land, but overall it's pretty forgettable stuff.
I love readin this author and the story telling is great but I really wanted more Christmas stories but still gave it a five star rating.
It's a good idea but bin is made in China and looks cheap so you'll be paying way too much for what you get.

I've had this for about six months now and it's held up surprisingly well to my toddler's abuse. She's thrown the pieces around, drooled on everything, and tried to chew on the rings constantly. Nothing has cracked or broken, which I appreciate. That said, the actual play value is pretty limited. My kid loses interest fast because the buttons just trigger short melodies instead of letting her actually make music. The stacking part is awkward since the whole thing is top-heavy and tips over easily. Removing the drum piece takes more force than a little one can manage alone. It's fine for what it is and definitely durable, but I wouldn't say it's a favorite toy in our house.
Great value and my guinea pig loves it. Packs down small but fluffs up nicely. Absorbs moisture really well and barely any smell.

This review in not for the movies which I consider them to be great. This review is for the product itself: I bought an used set in Amazon and the phantom of the Opera won't play in any of my HD DVD players HD-A20 HD-A30 and HD-D2. The Fugitive had pixel problems in all players and the same happened to the unforgiven. the only movie that was OK was The Last Samurai. Then I decided to buy a set that was new, thinking that the reason why the previous one, wouldn't play was because it was used and therefore somehow damaged. Well to my surprise the brand new one had exactly the same problem. therefore I don't recommend this set to any one. By the way all of the players have the latest 4.0 firmware update, so the players should not be the issue.
Very flimsy and hard to put it together. Once you mess the back carton board corner, it's impossible to fix it.

I grabbed this expecting a full follow-up to Never Surrender, but honestly it's barely a chapter. Took me maybe fifteen minutes to get through. That said, the writing itself is solid and it was nice checking back in with these characters. I just wish there had been more substance to it. For the price I was hoping for at least a short novella with some real plot progression. If you go in knowing it's basically a quick bonus scene you'll probably enjoy it fine, just manage your expectations.

i received this knife after some mishandling during shipping, it appears the original one was lost or stolen...but I digress. now on to the knife itself, when I opened the package I wasn't expecting very much but I was surprised to find a really good quality blade that wasn't chrome but a matt silver which is fine for me. the edge was very sharp out of the box and the case seemed to lock onto the blade securely. ive heard that you get what you pay for...but this knife is a great value for the low price.

Got this for my older 16 foot Tracker with a 75 horse Mariner and honestly wasn't expecting much for the price. Figured it was worth a gamble compared to the big name brands charging three times as much. Glad I rolled the dice. Hole shot is way better now, the bow stays down instead of pointing at the sky, and I'm on plane in half the distance it used to take. Cruising at wide open throttle feels planted and predictable, no more wandering back and forth like the hull used to do. Even handles wakes from other boats way smoother. Installation took maybe twenty minutes with basic hand tools. For what this costs versus the difference it makes, you really can't go wrong. Should've bought one years ago.

For under ten bucks this little spatula is honestly a great deal. The silicone tip is firm enough to actually push through thick peanut butter but soft enough to wipe jars clean without leaving residue behind. My only minor complaint is the handle feels a bit short for deeper containers, and I sometimes wish it had a bit more reach. That said, it's held up through multiple dishwasher cycles with no warping or fading. Perfect for the price point and gets the job done well.

Got my copy pretty quick with prime shipping, arrived in one of those padded mailers so the cover wasn't bent up or anything. Always a plus when you order paperbacks online. Started reading the same night and finished it over the weekend. This third installment really delivers if you've been following the series. The chemistry between the main couple feels solid at this point, though I wish there was a little more tension to keep things spicy. The drama with the secondary characters though? Absolutely wild. Some of those scenes had me staying up way past my bedtime. Not gonna lie, I expected a bit more from the ending, felt a little rushed. Still a solid read overall and I'll definitely pick up whatever comes next.
Bought these for my run, they stayed put all day.

I bought this for the room that my cats litter pan is in. The filter that came with the fan worked but I wanted to see if this would do any better. I was very surprised at the difference. Within about a hour the room was the freshest in the house. The room is 12x14 and there is a noticeable difference even in the adjoining rooms. I think even the cat has noticed.
Arrived two days early in a sturdy mailer with no bent corners. Cracked it open right away and was hooked by the second chapter. Already loaning it to my coworker.
They are real light and comfortable. My feet felt fine all day especially standing on concrete floors for the most part. Will by others in different colors.

These were perfect for me what we needed them for. There does seem to be very slight height differences but the widths were all the same for me. For the extremely low price they are a good value. They look nice and even when loaded with bath products have held up for me. Could they be nicer? Sure, but at this price point they're a good value.

I was bitten by the Spyder years ago, and have learned one valuable thing. If I see a Spyderco I like, I jump on it immediately, as the company discontinues models all of the time. Often, a model will return briefly in a limited edition "sprint run", and those generally sell out quickly because of a wide collector base. This model is small, tough, and insanely sharp. At this price, if you've considered collecting, this one is a good start. Legendary quality on a knife that may or may not be around for long.
Nice size for everyday essentials. Love the crossbody strap, though the zipper sticks sometimes. Great value overall.
Looks like pictured but my daughter doesn't like how much the puffy sleeves are sewed in. She complains that they bother her.

Got these for my border collie who's a picky eater and normally snubs store-bought treats. She literally drools when I open the bag now. The strips are easy to break into smaller bits for training sessions, and they don't crumble apart in my pocket like other jerky treats do. Minimal smell too, which I appreciate. I'd highly recommend these to anyone doing obedience work or agility training with their dog. Just keep the bag somewhere your pup can't reach it.
excellent but somewhat heavy 300g, to be an XT product

A very useful and even attractive help to us fun-sized people (i.e., short). It folds up by just lifting it at the handle shaped opening on top and opens into a step stool when you put it down. I use this in my bathroom and laundry room to reach stuff on high shelves and I really like it. Feels sturdy and well made and does not wobble.
My daughter loves these but after a few weeks the joints got really loose. One leg snapped at the hip and won't stay attached anymore. Fun concept but they can't hold up to regular kid play.

Gave up halfway through. The characters are flat and the plot jumps around like crazy. I'd only recommend this to hardcore sci-fi completionists who feel obligated to read everything in the genre. Everyone else, save your money.
Worth every penny. The vest and onesie combo is adorable.
Bought these for early morning snow removal in Minnesota. The grip on the fingers is fantastic, can actually hold the scraper without it slipping. Warm and completely dry inside. Worth every penny.

I Will Breathe Forbidden Series, Book 1 Regina Puckett Reviewed by Roy Murry, Author This Young Adult read is for the young at heart like them and me. It is fun, bizarre, and delightful. Some of the reasons I keep coming back to Regina's short novels In this adventure, Liberty, a young lady pilot of an airship, goes about her business of bartering items needed in her post end of times world. On one of her stops to deliver goods, she takes on the task of caring for a robot who has feelings. On a stop to get water fuel for her ship, she encounters other humans. The adventure begins, and Liberty breaks all of her dead father's rules by engaging the robot and the humans. She finds love, a trait she has been avoiding all her life. How this all comes about and how Liberty changes are the lessons learned in this novel; some that YA's should learn. An enjoyable quick read. The way I like it.
Bought this for my 8 year old daughter. The neck is not straight, it will not tune, and it is going back the same day it was delivered. Don't waste your time and money.

Upgrading to this saw after burning out a budget model was the right call. The variable speed control is smooth and actually responsive, unlike my old one. Vibration is minimal once I bolted it to a sturdy stand, and blade changes are quick once you get the hang of the mechanism. I've been cutting 3/4-inch hardwoods and it powers through without bogging down. The dust blower could be stronger though, I still end up brushing sawdust off my cut lines by hand fairly often. The table tilts easily and locks solid. Only real complaint is the LED light position isn't adjustable, so it doesn't always shine where I need it. Overall a solid mid-range machine that handles everything I throw at it.
I love the size and look of these things I have several in different colors. They are compact and easy to travel with. I have had no technical issues.

One ring mount fit the rail, the other didn't without excessive force, but backed it off the rail after a half inch to avoid damage to rail and / or mount ring. Requesting exchange & hoping for a dual fit on second attempt.

I'm not an audiophile. My golden ears from yesteryear have tarnished rather badly. I wanted a no-frills subwoofer to balance out my good-but-small bookshelf speakers. The first of these units arrived damaged: the grille had sheared off and could not be reattached. The company was perfect. They told me what to do and sent a replacement unit with no complaint or hassle. It came quickly. The instructions aren't the very clearest, but it didn't take long to figure it out and set it up. One complaint: whoever designed the connector array never, ever tried to connect anything to it. The connectors, even if they were well placed, are hard to use; but they're crammed together such that my not-very-big fingers had a heck of a time connecting the speakers. Much bad language was used. In terms of function, I can set the unit to make much more boom-boom than I want or to be almost silent - or anything in between, including a setting I like. The other speakers are routed through this unit, which ostensibly leads to better balance. Can't prove it by me, but the result seems fine.
This item did not work when plugged into my car slot. Tried both my cars and did not work. Wouldn't suggest anyone to waste their time on this.
Replaced my whole knife block. These look gorgeous and cut beautifully.
Really solid quality for the price. My dog looks adorable in it.

My boy cannot get enough of this. I`ve gone thru both of them already. () This toy is actually very durable, he just play with it that \_()_/ much. I live in a two story house and my kitty will drag it all the way upstairs to play. He runs and jumps until he's panting. Great exercise for my cat, and fun for me, too! I received this as a gift to share my experiences while using this product.

Been shooting with this for about four months now and I'm still finding new things to love. The autofocus is incredibly fast, even in tricky low light situations. Image quality blew me away right out of the box. Battery life gets me through a full day of shooting without worry. Worth every penny for serious photographers.

I was pretty skeptical about non-clumping litter at first, but after a month of using this stuff I'm completely converted. We've got three indoor cats and odor control has always been a nightmare. This literally absorbs the smell on contact. When the cats pee, the liquid just gets absorbed and you stir it around with the scoop to refresh it. Poop dries out fast and doesn't stink up the whole room like with traditional clay litter. I'd highly recommend this to anyone with multiple cats, apartment dwellers, or really anyone tired of scooping rock-hard clumps every single day. A thirty pound bag lasts me about five weeks with three cats, so the value is solid. Definitely repurchasing.

Got this for my Belgian Malinois who destroys every toy within 48 hours. Three months in and there's barely a scratch on it. For under fifteen bucks, I expected maybe a week of use, so this thing has already paid for itself ten times over. It's the only ball she can't puncture or chew apart, and trust me, she's tried. The thing basically rolls forever on grass, which keeps her sprinting and exhausted by the end of our park sessions. One heads-up though, it's solid and weighs more than you'd think, so don't full-send it at close range or it'll bounce off your dog's face. Roll it underhand and you're golden. Honestly best dog toy purchase I've made this year by far.

I finally got tired of my silver getting tarnished in a drawer and decided to try this instead. The anti-tarnish lining is the real winner here. I live in a humid climate and my silverware used to look dull after just a couple weeks. I've had pieces stored in this for over three months now and they still come out shiny with zero polishing needed. The compartments are well sized too. My larger soup spoons and serving forks actually fit without having to jam them in. The felt lining is soft enough that nothing scratches. My only minor complaint is the lid could be a bit sturdier, but for the price it does exactly what I need. Definitely recommend if you hate polishing silver as much as I do.

A time long gone, sadly. Inspiring tale of gentle discovery. Beautiful prose, honest conversation. Charley must have been a wonderful dog, too. His conversation is probably the most real of them all. I've had a standard poodle, and they do communicate very well.....
I have NO CLUE what happened the last 10 minutes of this movie. And the POINT was??? What a let down. BTW, -- the other ONE-STAR reviews? Believe them!
I've carried this little book in my purse daily for over a year. Pages still hold up great despite constant flipping. Really comforting prayers.

This is very informative on how the Brian works. I haven't read it all but so far it's been super informative for me. It's very interesting to learn bc not everyone's brains are developed the exact same and your brain controls ALOT! I recieved this free in exchange for an honest and unbiased review. So far I am impressed!

Great cheap pots. Dishwasher safe (no nasty exterior coating like other pots) Good nonstick interior all the way to the rim. Doesn't chip easily. I got two and use them both several times throughout the week. They are a great size and do whatever job I throw at them. I am going to subtract 1 star (although I would only subratct.5 if half-stars were possible) because of the lids. It's not the fit, they fit fine, make a great seal and everything. The problem is with the attachment of the handle, which creates a big empty space above the glass in the center of the lid which traps all kinds of nastiness when you wash it in the dishwasher. It's not too much of pain to clean, but it does require unscrewing the handle and removing it completely, which is inconvenient. Fix that one issue and this would be a 5 star cheap pot easily. (it is quite thin, though, maybe too thin for a gas burner, but works great on electric)
Got these for work and they're perfect. Would recommend to anyone on their feet all day.

Got this to replace my old print server and honestly it's just okay. The good news is it handles my Brother laser printer without any weird driver issues, which was my biggest worry. Print jobs go through reliably once everything is configured. What really bugs me though is the web interface. It's clunky and slow, and half the pages take forever to load. Changing any setting feels like a chore. I also wish there was a status LED that actually told you something useful, the lights are there but good luck figuring out what they mean without the manual open. For basic printing it does the job fine. If you need anything more advanced or want a smooth setup experience, probably look elsewhere.

It is a very nice story. It is funny and loving. Sometime I get mad at men who are so stubborn, but I guess we women are that way sometime to. It is romance and that is what the book is about and it is good.

Good character development, good story line and easy to read. I enjoyed all three books in the set. I would have liked each to have been easier to believe in parts of the stories. I have no direct knowledge of the FBI, however some parts of the stories just worked out too easily thus making them suspect instead of normal.

The quick-lace system on these is genuinely convenient and the grip is fantastic on trails. But the arch support is way too aggressive for my flat feet. After a short hike my arches were cramping badly. Had to return them. If you have high arches these might be perfect, but they just don't work for my foot shape at all.
Honestly didn't think it'd handle all the pet hair in our house, but it does a solid job. Not perfect on stairs, but better than I expected for the price.
I like to have bene bac on hand just in case my rabbit needs it. I like this large syringe much better than the little tubes.
Exceeded my expectations honestly. Mounted it in five minutes and feels way more solid than the old one I replaced.
Perfect fit. Great for when you want to store in refrigerator. Good seal.
We love these lights. Seem to be good quality and they're always a subject of interest when we have guests. Everyone loves them.

Been using this case for about six months now and it's held up really well. Dropped my phone on concrete twice and neither the case nor the screen took any damage. The buttons still click perfectly and the ports haven't stretched out. It's slim enough to slide in my pocket but tough enough that I don't worry about it. Took on a little discoloration around the edges but nothing major. Solid buy overall.
Engrossing loving tale of an abandoned child who became a famous violinist in the time of Vivaldi. The story of her coming to find her self and her place in old Venice.

The binding on these softcover editions is surprisingly solid for the price. Pages don't fall out when you open it flat, and the cover has a nice matte finish that doesn't show fingerprints. Text is cleanly printed with decent margins. Not a collector's item but a sturdy everyday reader that holds up well.

This is the perfect Holiday short story read. I Loved this story as I always love Deanna Chase's writing. The relations ship between Jade and Kane is Awesome and I also enjoy the relationships between the friends and coven members.

I needed to update my small business website but couldn't afford to hire a developer, so I figured I'd try teaching myself. Honestly, I was shocked at how fast I picked things up with this guide. The examples are super practical, and I actually had a working page with links and images by the afternoon. It doesn't drown you in jargon or unnecessary theory, it just gets you coding right away. I'd absolutely recommend this to small business owners, bloggers, or really anyone who wants a quick crash course without committing to a full semester-long class. My only minor complaint is I wish it had a bit more on forms, but for what it promises, it absolutely delivers. Worth every penny.

I really enjoyed the tension between the two leads, they're both stubborn to a fault and it makes every scene feel charged. The author clearly knows how to write banter that actually bites instead of the usual flat dialogue you find in a lot of romance picks. My one gripe is the pacing in the middle. There's a stretch where the same argument gets rehashed three chapters in a row and I almost put it down. Glad I stuck with it though because the last quarter really delivers. The spicy scenes are well done and feel earned, not rushed. Would've liked more resolution on the side plot with the sister but overall a solid read. I'll definitely check out whatever this author puts out next.
Incredible dogfight sequences for under ten bucks. The aerial combat footage alone is worth twice the price. Any aviation fan needs this in their collection.
this holster is a great paddle holster. puts the gun in a good position and holds it snug. I would buy it again.

Bought this to replace the Canon OEM cap when I lose it...it's just a matter of when. Pinch mechanism works well and fits just as well as original. Bought this for a Canon 18-135mm STM lens and would certainly recommend.

The how to book on money laundering. A long and rich history of how to get rich at the expense of the public. Slick Willie and his bride have mastered the art of political graft and on how to not get prosecuted.
Great deal for how many you get. Slide right into my 2020 rail with zero wiggle. Stocked up.
Awesome Scooby-Doo Lego set for the price. My daughter adores it.

I really enjoyed the premise of this book. The idea of a letter meant to be opened after death being read early is such a compelling hook, and Moriarty does a great job building tension around what the contents might be. The pacing started strong and kept me turning pages for the first half. What frustrated me was how the male characters were written. They felt like cardboard cutouts with no real depth or distinct personalities. Every husband in the story blurred together, and I found myself flipping back trying to remember which man belonged to which storyline. The female leads carried the book, but the male cast needed way more development.

Ceony thought that the danger was over. She saved the magician she was apprenticing and put an end to the threat. She didn't expect to be targeted for revenge for what she did. She also didn't expect to have to hide a budding relationship with her mentor from everybody around her. I have to be very honest, when I first started reading this book, I was trying to figure out why there was a need for sequels. Then it all hit at once and I fell into the story without another thought. It may have a very slow start, but the buildup behind it is amazing! The narrator was as amazing in this as she was the first book. I really adored her. I'd love to listen to more books by this narrator. The story was good itself, but she enhanced it even more and brought life to all of the characters. I do recommend this book very strongly. However, I recommend that the reader read the first book before picking this book up. Many references are made to the first book that may cause a reader who didn't read it to be very confused and not as interested in the story as they would be if they read the first.

Bought this for a last-minute baby shower and was honestly shocked at the quality for the price. I bought my sg3 brand new about 5/6 yrs ago, it still works like a champ til this day. Played a round this past Saturday. It doesn't have all the bells and whistles like the newer models, but its accurate, links to the satellite in 1 or 2 mins, gives me carried yardage, hazard yardage (front and carry), distance to middle, front and back of greens, you can use the small joystick to place the pin in appropriate place on green for more accurated pin yardage. It has a score feature, but it takes to long to get to the next hole, so I have this feature turned off. All in all, I LOVE IT, they don't make this model anymore, but I bet the refurbished sg3 is just as good, unless your into color screens, touch screens and other things that slows your game down. This one gives you what you need, accurate yardage to hazards, lay ups and pin, so all you need is good golf shot. Oh yea, it takes 2 AA batteries that last approximately 2 rounds and you pay for the courses, but its a minimal yearly subscription and the website is very easy to use and downloads or quik

The Glimpsing is an intriguing and haunting tale of revenge, love, hope, faith, courage, and the age-old battle of good vs. evil. The author creates a very Twilight Zone atmosphere from the beginning of the book, with nods to Alfred Hitchcock, Rod Serling and Stephen King. Although I wouldn't classify it as a horror story, it incorporates the psychological horror and suspense for which the above three are recognized as masters. Portia Childress, brought up by a mother who has the ability to "glimpse", see into the world just beyond our world, wants the one thing she can't find in her relationships with men: true love. How she deals with this unfulfilled need is the center of this tale. The mysterious painting reminds me of aspects of "The Picture of Dorian Gray" and subsequent stories involving mysterious, "changing" paintings but this has its own unique flavor. It is easy to identify with Portia's innocence and need but more difficult to understand where she went as result of it. Gabrielle, in spite of her own love for Jack, tries to remain loyal to Portia and to love her best friend. Jack's increasing feelings for Gabrielle only complicate the situation, bringing about some of the ensuing horrors. My favorite character in this story, though a minor one was Jack's housekeeper, Janice. She has the wisdom to get an idea of what is going on after Jack first receives the mysterious painting, offers Gabrielle a way of dealing with her own guilt and ultimately is willing to risk dealing with something she is worried is beyond her ability to handle, because of her belief in doing the right thing. This was a refreshing read, standing out among the current "horror" stories that I have read with its unique presentation of that battle between good and evil. Well worth the time invested in reading the book. Unfortunately because it would give away spoilers, I can't go into the details of the unique perspective presented in this story, but I think any fan of the horror/supernatural realm would enjoy the book

Can't beat this for the money. I've seen similar poles going for twice the price that aren't half as sturdy. The telescoping sections lock tight with no wobble, and the push-button mechanism lines up perfectly every single time. No fighting to get segments aligned like cheaper brands. Wall thickness is solid, doesn't flex under load. Used it for clearing brush and reaching high limbs around my property. Fits all my attachments with no issues. Got this on a Kindle deal for like two bucks and honestly it punched way above what I paid. This is the third Bill Travis book and the guy just keeps getting better. Bill heads back to his old stomping grounds to attend a friend's funeral and walks straight into a mess that had me flipping pages way past bedtime. What I really like is how Wier doesn't waste your time with filler, every chapter moves the story forward. For under three dollars you're getting a solid mystery with real heart and some genuinely funny moments scattered throughout. Already grabbed the next one. These books are seriously underrated.

In an attempt to save some money by eating out less, I went on a quest for the best way to bake a frozen pizza (which puts me back $5 vs the $20 for takeout). I am not a fan of placing the frozen pizza directly on the oven rack. The hassle of having to remove and wash the rack after each use (as well having to clean the cheese that drips off onto the oven floor) made this a non-option. Pizza sheets for baking result in a soggy crust. I tried a ceramic pizza stone and precisely followed the directions of preheating then placing the frozen pizza onto it for baking. The outer edges were nice and crispy but the center was always soggy. Also any cheese that got onto the stone was an ordeal to wash off. Then I saw this baking rack... I did follow the advice of previous reviews and measured my baking sheet to make sure it fit. I also line the baking sheet with some foil for easy clean up. This was the perfect solution!!! The frozen pizzas turn out evenly cooked and no soggy middle!! In the time it takes to call a restaurant then drive out to pick up the pizza (or have it delivered), I can have one made for 1/4 the price (and serve it piping hot directly from the oven) at home!!! I also made some baked sweet potato fries on this rack and they turned out great too!!! Only advice is to not overload it or the fries will not evenly cook.

I went into this series expecting a pretty standard romantic comedy setup, but it genuinely surprised me. The pacing is way better than I anticipated, and the emotional moments actually land instead of feeling forced. I'd watched the original OVA years ago and was worried this longer adaptation would drag things out or lose the charm. Instead it expands on the supporting cast in ways that made me care about characters I barely noticed before. The animation quality holds up beautifully too, especially during the fantasy sequences. My only gripe is I wish there were more episodes because by the finale I wasn't ready to say goodbye to these characters. Easily exceeded what I was hoping for. If you're on the fence, just grab it.

Grabbed a six-pack of these on clearance about eight months ago when my local store was clearing shelf space. Figured at that price, even if they were mediocre, no big loss. Well, I've got them scattered all over the house now, porch, kitchen, garage, bathroom, and every single one is still going strong. The two on my back porch have survived rain, humidity, and temp swings from freezing to triple digits without flickering or dimming. What really surprised me is the build quality. Most cheap LEDs feel like flimsy plastic, but these have some real weight to them and that ceramic section actually pulls heat away from the diodes. No buzzing, instant on, consistent color temp across all six. Honestly better than name brands I've paid triple for. Stocking up next time I'm there.

Blasphemy is the second book featuring Wynam Ford (now a private detective). In this book, Ford infiltrates a government-run facility which has built the world's largest particle accelerator for a mere $40 billion. (Please suspend your disbelief.) I like the way Preston has worked together several subplots which all culminate at the end of the book for a complex climax. I am also a fan of Preston's easy writing style--it's good for a quick read. I am not a fan of Preston's portrayal of Born Again Christians--I think it's a bit over-the-top, though I guess it's a reasonable depiction of a very small percentage of rapture-ready Christians. Also, Preston is clearly not a physicist, but I guess not everyone can be perfect.:) Overall, I think Blasphemy was good for a quick sci-fi/techno read, but shouldn't be taken too seriously. It's a beach-book.
